articles.title: At least 12 people shot at an Ohio festival and a search for suspects is still ongoing, police say
articles.summary: Gunfire erupted Saturday near a busy street festival in Ohio, wounding at least 12 people and sending some eventgoers scrambling for cover while others rushed to help the victims.
articles.titleZh: 俄亥俄州音乐节至少有 12 人遭枪击，警方称仍在搜寻嫌疑人
articles.summaryZh: 周六，俄亥俄州一个繁忙的街头节日附近爆发了枪声，造成至少 12 人受伤，一些参加活动的人争先恐后地寻找掩护，而其他人则急忙帮助受害者。

contentParagraphs.en: The Agnes Reynolds Jackson Arboretum is shown where multiple people were shot at a community festival Saturday, June 6, 2026, in Toledo, Ohio.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 6 日星期六，俄亥俄州托莱多，艾格尼丝·雷诺兹·杰克逊植物园在一次社区节日上遭到枪击。

contentParagraphs.en: Gunfire erupted Saturday near a busy street festival in Ohio, wounding at least 12 people and sending some eventgoers scrambling for cover while others rushed to help the victims.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周六，俄亥俄州一个繁忙的街头节日附近爆发了枪声，造成至少 12 人受伤，一些参加活动的人争先恐后地寻找掩护，而其他人则急忙帮助受害者。

contentParagraphs.en: No suspects were in custody hours afterward, Toledo Deputy Police Chief Joe Heffernan said, and officials urged people who were at the festival to come forward with any photos or videos on their phones for possible leads.
contentParagraphs.zh: 托莱多副警察局长乔·赫弗南 (Joe Heffernan) 表示，几小时后没有嫌疑人被拘留，官员们敦促参加音乐节的人们提供手机上的照片或视频，以寻找可能的线索。

contentParagraphs.en: The shooting happened near the Old West End Festival, an annual gathering of live music and home tours.
contentParagraphs.zh: 枪击事件发生在老西区音乐节附近，这是一年一度的现场音乐和家庭巡演聚会。

contentParagraphs.en: Heffernan said it appeared that at least two people fired weapons and they were "probably shooting at each other."
contentParagraphs.zh: 赫弗南说，至少有两人开枪，“可能是在互相射击”。

contentParagraphs.en: Two of the victims were in critical condition, Heffernan added. The ages of the victims ranged from 14 to 61, with most of them in their early 20s.
contentParagraphs.zh: 赫弗南补充说，其中两名受害者情况危急。受害者年龄从14岁到61岁不等，其中大多数20岁出头。

contentParagraphs.en: "I am deeply concerned about the situation in Toledo tonight," Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine said in a statement. "Summer festivals should be safe spaces for families to spend time together without fear of violence."
contentParagraphs.zh: 俄亥俄州州长迈克·德温在一份声明中说：“我对今晚托莱多的局势深感担忧。” “夏季节日应该是家庭共度时光的安全场所，而不必担心暴力。”

contentParagraphs.en: Multiple videos posted to social media showed people running amid the sound of gunshots and emergency officials tending to others who appeared wounded.
contentParagraphs.zh: 社交媒体上发布的多段视频显示，人们在枪声中奔跑，应急官员正在照顾其他似乎受伤的人。

contentParagraphs.en: Fire Chief Allison Armstrong said it was difficult to get to the hospital due to closed roads and traffic from people leaving the festival, but emergency responders were able to transport all patients from the scene within an hour.
contentParagraphs.zh: 消防队长艾利森·阿姆斯特朗表示，由于道路封闭和人们离开节日的交通，很难到达医院，但紧急救援人员能够在一小时内将所有患者从现场转移出去。

contentParagraphs.en: Kevin Berry was sitting in the neighborhood arboretum listening to live music with friends when he heard a handful of gunshots ring out.
contentParagraphs.zh: 凯文·贝里 (Kevin Berry) 正和朋友坐在附近的植物园里听现场音乐，突然听到几声枪响。

contentParagraphs.en: "Everybody hit the deck," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “每个人都上台了，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: When Berry looked back up, he saw a gun being tossed to the ground less than 50 feet (15 meters) away from him. Officers who were already on site for the festival responded immediately.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当贝里回头看时，他看到一把枪被扔在距离他不到 50 英尺（15 米）的地方。已经在现场参加节日的官员立即做出了回应。

contentParagraphs.en: Berry, who has medical training and served in the Navy, walked around looking for anyone who might need help and saw at least five people with gunshot wounds.
contentParagraphs.zh: 贝里接受过医疗培训并在海军服役，他四处寻找可能需要帮助的人，发现至少有五人受枪伤。

contentParagraphs.en: "The folks who were hit were spread out around the arboretum area," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “被击中的人分散在植物园周围，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: The Old West End Festival is a two-day celebration in Toledo's historic district that includes live music, food vendors, home tours and shopping. Berry described it as the "kick-off to Toledo's summer festival season."
contentParagraphs.zh: 老西区节是在托莱多历史街区举行的为期两天的庆祝活动，包括现场音乐、食品摊贩、家庭旅游和购物。贝里将其描述为“托莱多夏季节日季的开始”。

contentParagraphs.en: George Kral, safety director for the city, said officials were discussing with organizers whether it would continue through the weekend.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该市安全总监乔治·克拉尔(George Kral)表示，官员们正在与组织者讨论活动是否会持续到周末。

contentParagraphs.en: "This is one of the most iconic festivals in Toledo," he said, "and it's a shame that something like this had to ruin it."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这是托莱多最具标志性的节日之一，”他说，“很遗憾这样的事情不得不毁掉它。”

articles.titleZh: “政治暴力的驱动者”：人工智能的迅猛发展如何助长反科技极端主义
articles.summaryZh:
追随早期技术悲观主义激进分子的脚步，对人工智能的强烈抵制正在转向极端主义

订阅美国突发新闻时事通讯电子邮件

今年早些时候，一名来自德克萨斯州的 20 岁男子因涉嫌试图烧毁 OpenAI 总部而被捕

contentParagraphs.en: When a 20-year-old man from Texas was arrested earlier this year for allegedly trying to burn down OpenAI’s headquarters and Sam Altman’s house, authorities found an anti-AI manifesto alongside his lighter and a jug of kerosene. It was one of a spate of attacks that has caused alarm among researchers, the tech industry and law enforcement about the rise of anti-tech extremism.In April, an Italian “nature pilled” Instagram influencer was arrested in Rome and charged with plotting a series of anti-tech attacks that took inspiration from Ted “The Unabomber” Kaczynski. Two self-described “ecofascists” that carried out a deadly anti-Muslim attack on a mosque in San Diego last month also cited “AI slop” and JD Vance’s ties to Palantir as motivations for their violence in their manifesto. An Indianapolis city councilor woke up earlier this year to gunshots being fired into his home before finding a note that read “NO DATA CENTERS”.The growing public backlash to the tech industry’s rapid rollout of artificial intelligence has taken many, mostly-non violent forms such as local communities organizing against datacenters and political candidates promising increased oversight. Yet at the fringes, researchers say grievances against the AI industry and its leaders are animating old violent extremist movements and fomenting new ones.“AI is becoming this driver of political violence, and that’s a very new phenomenon,” said Jordyn Abrams, a researcher at the Program on Extremism at George Washington University.While much of the early public discussion around generative AI and extremism focused on how malign actors like terrorist groups could misuse products such as ChatGPT for propaganda purposes or plotting attacks, there is more recent attention given to how the AI industry as a whole can radicalize people. What motivates someone to extremist violence might not be a conversation with a chatbot, researchers say, but the society-wide disruption, narrative of existential threat and lack of accountability that has come with the AI boom.In the same way that AI has come to pervade many facets of modern life, the technology has also filtered into the way that extremists think about the world. Whether it is violent anti-government groups opposing mass surveillance, ecofascists with environmental grievances, neo-Nazi accelerationists bent on collapsing critical tech infrastructure or the man who allegedly targeted Altman’s house worried about superpowerful artificial intelligence destroying humanity, AI has become a fixation across the extremist spectrum.“It really transcends these left-right dichotomies,” said Yannick Veilleux-Lepage, an associate professor at the Royal Military College of Canada. “We’re seeing a lot of different groups, a lot of different ideologies being framed through a lens of anti-AI.”There isn’t time for people to build resilience’The modern anti-tech movement has a long lineage. Periods of technological change are historically accompanied by backlash from the people most affected, with researchers often pointing to the early 19th-century luddite rebellion of British textile workers smashing automated knitting machines as they demanded more labor rights. The next 200 years brought waves of violent labor disputes and political violence that accompanied tech’s market disruptions, uneven accumulation of wealth and disenfranchisement of workers.In the 1990s, there was cultural pushback against the rise of the personal computer and the fear of how it would disrupt society. Common complaints included fears of replacing human workers, environmental harm and crumbling healthy social structures.“Haven’t you heard? It wants your job. It peddles you smut. It corrupts your kids. It’s cold, sterile, inhuman. Suddenly, it’s okay to hate your computer,” read a New York Magazine cover story from 1995 on the “New Luddites”.The same year as New York Magazine ran its cover story, the Washington Post and the New York Times published the Unabomber’s anti-tech manifesto, a 35,000-word screed against industrial society that has proliferated online in the years since and become the closest thing that anti-tech extremism has to a foundational text.What separates anti-AI extremism from these previous waves of tech backlash, researchers say, is partly the speed and scale of how AI is bringing about economic, social and political change.“Not only are these whole-of-society changes and not only are they really disruptive, they’re happening really quickly,” Veilleux-Lepage said. “There isn’t time for people to build resilience or to inoculate themselves from these changes”.The AI industry’s longstanding talking points – that the technology will revolutionize the world, if not end it – also lend themselves to a radicalizing narrative that AI poses an existential threat and must be stopped at all costs. When Veilleux-LePage gives talks to policymakers about anti-tech extremism, one of his slides simply features a series of quotes from CEOs.“In order to radicalize people, you don’t actually need to have theorists or ideologues that are calling people to violence against AI, because the tech CEOs are doing a pretty good case,” Veilleux-LePage said.‘I expect some really bad stuff to happen’Altman has often framed the changes AI will bring as something that may be difficult, but is ultimately both positive – above all, he describes the change as inevitable.“I expect some really bad stuff to happen because of the technology which also has happened with previous technologies,” Altman said on venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz’s podcast last year.While tech CEOs are publicly optimistic about the resilience of society and the change that AI will bring about, it is also clear that they are privately concerned with the threat of political violence. Spending on personal security for executives has ballooned over the past five years amid incidents such as the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, while tech leaders
contentParagraphs.zh: 今年早些时候，一名来自德克萨斯州的 20 岁男子因涉嫌试图烧毁 OpenAI 总部和 Sam Altman 的房子而被捕，当局在他的打火机和一壶煤油旁边发现了一份反人工智能宣言。这是一系列袭击事件之一，引起了研究人员、科技行业和执法部门对反科技极端主义崛起的警惕。 今年 4 月，一位意大利“自然破坏”Instagram 网红在罗马被捕，被指控策划一系列反科技袭击，其灵感来自“大学炸弹客”特德·卡钦斯基 (Ted “The Unabomber” Kaczynski)。上个月，两名自称为“生态法西斯主义者”的人对圣地亚哥一座清真寺发动了致命的反穆斯林袭击，他们还在宣言中将“AI slop”和 JD Vance 与 Palantir 的关系作为他们实施暴力的动机。今年早些时候，印第安纳波利斯市的一名市议员在家中听到枪声，醒来后发现了一张写着“没有数据中心”的纸条。公众对科技行业人工智能快速推广的强烈抵制采取了多种形式，大多是非暴力形式，例如当地社区组织起来反对数据中心，政治候选人承诺加强监管。然而，在边缘，研究人员表示，对人工智能行业及其领导人的不满正在激发旧的暴力极端主义运动，并煽动新的暴力极端主义运动。“人工智能正在成为政治暴力的驱动力，这是一个非常新的现象，”乔治华盛顿大学极端主义项目研究员乔丁·艾布拉姆斯说。虽然围绕生成人工智能和极端主义的早期公众讨论大多集中在恐怖组织等恶意行为者如何滥用人工智能诸如 ChatGPT 等用于宣传目的或策划攻击的产品，最近人们越来越关注人工智能行业作为一个整体如何使人们变得激进。研究人员表示，促使某人实施极端主义暴力的因素可能不是与聊天机器人的对话，而是人工智能繁荣带来的全社会的破坏、对生存威胁的叙述以及缺乏问责制。就像人工智能已经渗透到现代生活的许多方面一样，这项技术也渗透到了极端分子看待世界的方式中。无论是反对大规模监视的暴力反政府团体、对环境不满的生态法西斯分子、一心要摧毁关键技术基础设施的新纳粹加速主义者，还是据称以奥特曼的房子为目标的男子担心超强人工智能会毁灭人类，人工智能已经成为整个极端主义谱系的焦点。 “我们看到很多不同的群体、很多不同的意识形态都通过反人工智能的视角来构建。”“人们没有时间建立适应力”现代反科技运动有着悠久的历史。从历史上看，技术变革时期总是伴随着受影响最严重的人们的强烈反对，研究人员经常指出 19 世纪初英国纺织工人因要求更多劳工权利而砸毁自动针织机的卢德叛乱。接下来的 200 年，伴随着科技市场的混乱、财富积累的不平衡和工人权利被剥夺，出现了一波又一波的暴力劳资纠纷和政治暴力。 20 世纪 90 年代，人们对​​个人电脑的兴起存在文化抵制，并担心它会扰乱社会。常见的抱怨包括担心取代人类工人、环境破坏和崩溃的健康社会结构。“你没听说过吗？它想要你的工作。它兜售你的色情内容。它腐蚀你的孩子。它冷酷、无菌、不人道。突然间，你可以讨厌你的电脑了。”1995 年《纽约杂志》的封面故事《新勒德分子》上这样写道。在《纽约杂志》刊登封面故事的同一年，《华盛顿邮报》和《纽约时报》发表了Unabomber 的反科技宣言是一篇 35,000 字的反对工业社会的长篇大论，多年来在网上激增，成为反科技极端主义最接近基础文本的东西。研究人员表示，反人工智能极端主义与之前的科技反弹浪潮的区别部分在于人工智能带来经济、社会和政治变革的速度和规模。它们真的具有破坏性吗？它们发生得非常快，”维勒克斯-勒帕吉说。 “人们没有时间建立适应力或让自己免受这些变化的影响”。人工智能行业长期以来的话题——这项技术即使不能终结世界，也将彻底改变世界——也导致了一种激进的说法，即人工智能构成了生存威胁，必须不惜一切代价阻止。当 Veilleux-LePage 与政策制定者就反科技极端主义进行演讲时，他的一张幻灯片只是简单地引用了首席执行官的一系列引言。 Veilleux-LePage 说：“为了使人们变得激进，你实际上不需要号召人们对人工智能采取暴力的理论家或理论家，因为科技首席执行官们正在做一个很好的案例。”“我预计会发生一些非常糟糕的事情。”Altman 经常将人工智能将带来的变化描述为可能很困难，但最终都是积极的——最重要的是，他将这种变化描述为不可避免的。“我预计会发生一些非常糟糕的事情，因为以前的技术也发生过这种情况，”奥特曼去年在风险投资公司 Andreessen Horowitz 的播客上说道。虽然科技首席执行官们公开对社会的弹性和人工智能将带来的变化持乐观态度，但很明显，他们私下里也担心政治暴力的威胁。过去五年，由于联合医疗保健首席执行官布莱恩·汤普森（Brian Thompson）被杀等事件，高管的个人安全支出激增，而科技领导者

contentParagraphs.en: NPR's Eyder Peralta speaks with immunologist Nicole Baumgarth about why ticks are spreading to new regions, and what this increase in ticks could mean for the spread of Lyme disease.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Eyder Peralta 与免疫学家 Nicole Baumgarth 讨论了蜱虫为何传播到新地区，以及蜱虫数量的增加对莱姆病的传播意味着什么。

contentParagraphs.en: EYDER PERALTA, HOST: New data from the CDC suggest ticks are spreading farther than their normal regions in the U.S., and experts are worried that could also mean an increase in tick-borne illnesses like Lyme disease. Joining us now to explain more is Nicole Baumgarth. She's a professor of infectious diseases and director of Johns Hopkins University's Lyme and Tick-Borne Disease Research and Education Institute. Professor, thank you for being here.NICOLE BAUMGARTH: Yeah. Thanks for having me.PERALTA: How are tick-related incidents tracked?BAUMGARTH: They are usually tracked by the health departments. So every state department has a requirement for most tick-borne illnesses to be reported. So if you go to a local GP's office, if you have a tick-borne illness such as Lyme disease, your health professionals are supposed to report it to the health department. And they will collate the data and then send it to the Centers for Disease Control.PERALTA: And so the CDC reported an overall increase in incidents, but now things look more stable. Still, as we said, ticks seem to be spreading beyond where they are typically found this time of year. Any idea why?BAUMGARTH: So we have seen over the last three decades an increasing spread of ticks. This has to do with climate change. As the climate, in particular in the northern hemisphere, is warming, ticks can spread more north. So, for example, 20 years ago, there were really no overwintering ticks in the south of Canada. Now we have endemic populations of ticks, and they can transmit Lyme disease there. We also live closer to areas where ticks like to live. And we have a loss of biodiversity, meaning certain species that ticks like to feed on, such as deer and the white-footed mouse in the Northeastern United States, have expanded. And therefore the ability for ticks to eat more, and therefore they will expand as a population.PERALTA: So, Professor, I mean, I think the thing that most worry about is Lyme disease because, you know, it can cause fever and paralysis among other things. What do we know about its prevalence in the U.S. and globally?BAUMGARTH: The prevalence of Lyme disease is also on the rise, just as the population of ticks are increasing. In the U.S., we are now seeing - I mean, estimates are about 476,000 cases per year of Lyme disease. And it's really important to point out that these numbers are really likely incorrect because if you live in an area where there's a lot of Lyme disease going on, usually the doctor doesn't bother anymore to send this information to the CDC, so there are way less reported cases than we know are happening.PERALTA: What are you going to be looking for in the coming months to assess how much of a problem ticks will be in the future?BAUMGARTH: Yeah. We are particular worried about another tick species - as if we don't have enough with the Ixodes ticks, the black-legged tick that causes Lyme disease - and that is the Amblyomma tick, or the lone star tick. These are very aggressive ticks, even though they're very small. We have seen them expanding geographically. And what we're worried about with this tick is that they are likely the cause of Alpha-gal syndrome, this red meat allergy. And so we are looking at that as a sort of indicator of the tick population expanding and this Amblyomma tick population getting a foothold and taking over.PERALTA: That's Nicole Baumgarth. She's a professor of infectious diseases at Johns Hopkins University. Professor, thank you for talking with me.BAUMGARTH: Thank you very much.(SOUNDBITE OF MUSIC)
contentParagraphs.zh: 主持人艾德·佩拉尔塔：疾病预防控制中心的新数据表明，蜱虫在美国的传播范围比正常区域更远，专家担心这也可能意味着莱姆病等蜱传疾病的增加。妮可·鲍姆加斯 (Nicole Baumgarth) 现在加入我们进行更多解释。她是传染病学教授，也是约翰·霍普金斯大学莱姆病和蜱传疾病研究与教育研究所所长。教授，谢谢您来到这里。 妮可·鲍姆加斯： 是的。感谢您邀请我。 PERALTA：如何跟踪蜱虫相关事件？ BAUMGARTH：通常由卫生部门跟踪。因此，每个州部门都要求报告大多数蜱传疾病。因此，如果您去当地的全科医生办公室，如果您患有蜱传疾病，例如莱姆病，您的健康专业人员应该向卫生部门报告。他们将整理数据，然后将其发送给疾病控制中心。 佩拉尔塔：因此，疾病预防控制中心报告的事件总体有所增加，但现在情况看起来更加稳定。尽管如此，正如我们所说，蜱虫似乎正在蔓延到每年这个时候它们通常出现的地方之外。知道为什么吗？鲍姆加斯：过去三十年来，我们看到蜱虫的传播不断扩大。这与气候变化有关。随着气候变暖，特别是北半球的气候变暖，蜱虫可以向更北的地方传播。所以，举例来说，20年前，加拿大南部确实没有越冬的蜱虫。现在我们有地方性蜱虫种群，它们可以在那里传播莱姆病。我们住的地方也离蜱虫喜欢居住的地方更近。我们还丧失了生物多样性，这意味着某些蜱虫喜欢捕食的物种，例如美国东北部的鹿和白足鼠，已经扩大了。因此，蜱虫能够吃得更多，因此它们的种群数量将会扩大。 佩拉尔塔：所以，教授，我的意思是，我认为最担心的是莱姆病，因为，你知道，它会导致发烧和瘫痪等。我们对其在美国和全球的流行情况了解多少？鲍姆加斯：莱姆病的流行率也在上升，就像蜱虫数量在增加一样。在美国，我们现在看到——我的意思是，估计每年约有 476,000 例莱姆病病例。需要指出的是，这些数字确实很可能是不正确的，因为如果你生活在莱姆病流行的地区，通常医生不会再费心将这些信息发送给疾病预防控制中心，因此报告的病例比我们所知的要少得多。 佩拉尔塔：在接下来的几个月里，你会寻找什么来评估未来蜱虫问题的严重程度？鲍姆加斯：是的。我们特别担心另一种蜱虫——就好像我们没有足够的蜱虫蜱虫（引起莱姆病的黑腿蜱虫）——那就是 Amblyomma 蜱虫或孤星蜱虫。尽管它们很小，但它们是非常具有攻击性的蜱虫。我们看到它们在地理上不断扩张。我们担心这种蜱虫很可能是阿尔法半乳糖综合症（一种红肉过敏）的病因。因此，我们将其视为蜱虫种群扩张以及 Amblyomma 蜱虫种群获得立足点并接管的一种指标。 PERALTA： 那是 Nicole Baumgarth。她是约翰·霍普金斯大学传染病学教授。教授，谢谢您与我交谈。鲍姆加斯：非常感谢您。（音乐原声片段）

articles.title: When U.S. foreign aid changed, AIDS workers in Africa felt it
articles.summary: In South Africa and Mozambique, health care providers say cancellation or redirection of U.S. PEPFAR funding under the Trump administration have already endangered vulnerable people and cost lives.
articles.titleZh: 当美国对外援助发生变化时，非洲的艾滋病工作者也感受到了
articles.summaryZh: 在南非和莫桑比克，医疗保健提供者表示，特朗普政府取消或重新调整美国总统防治艾滋病紧急救援计划（PEPFAR）资金已经危及弱势群体并造成生命损失。

contentParagraphs.en: People queue outside the Unjani Clinic in Braamfischerville, Soweto, Johannesburg, South Africa on May 25, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 5 月 25 日，人们在南非约翰内斯堡索韦托 Braamfischerville 的 Unjani 诊所外排队。

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: This essay first appeared in the Up First newsletter. Sign up here.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本文首次发表于 Up First 时事通讯。在这里注册。

contentParagraphs.en: It can be hard to remember what the HIV/AIDS epidemic in Africa looked like decades ago: Hospitals across the continent were overwhelmed with young men and women, dying excruciating deaths.
contentParagraphs.zh: 人们很难回忆起几十年前非洲的艾滋病毒/艾滋病流行情况：整个非洲大陆的医院挤满了年轻男女，痛苦不堪地死去。

contentParagraphs.en: South Africa was at the center of the epidemic. Activist Lucky Mazibuko remembers, vividly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 南非是疫情的中心。活动家幸运·马兹布科 (Lucky Mazibuko) 对此记忆犹新。

contentParagraphs.en: He told me that at the time, the country "was filled with the stench of death."
contentParagraphs.zh: 他告诉我，当时这个国家“充满了死亡的恶臭”。

contentParagraphs.en: It seemed, he went on to say, that there would be no end to the suffering.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他接着说，痛苦似乎永无止境。

contentParagraphs.en: "There was no hope, there was basically no light," he told me. "And even if there was a light at the end of the tunnel, it looked like that of an oncoming train."
contentParagraphs.zh: “没有希望，基本上没有光明，”他告诉我。 “即使隧道尽头有一盏灯，它看起来也像是一列迎面而来的火车。”

contentParagraphs.en: The Esselen Clinic, which is situated on the same street as the WITS RHI Women's Health Clinic continues to operate with patients lining up outside on Esselen street, Hillbrow, Johannesburg, South Africa on May 25, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 5 月 25 日，与 WITS RHI 妇女健康诊所位于同一条街上的埃塞伦诊所继续营业，患者在南非约翰内斯堡希尔布罗埃塞伦街外排队。

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: PEPFAR changed everything — across the continent.
contentParagraphs.zh: PEPFAR 改变了整个非洲大陆的一切。

contentParagraphs.en: President George W. Bush announced the President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief, or PEPFAR, in January 2003. The program is often cited as the most effective public health campaign ever, and is estimated by the State Department to have saved roughly 26 million lives since its inception. And for decades, the program enjoyed widespread bipartisan support.
contentParagraphs.zh: 乔治·W·布什总统于 2003 年 1 月宣布了总统艾滋病紧急救援计划 (PEPFAR)。该计划经常被认为是有史以来最有效的公共卫生运动，据国务院估计，自启动以来已挽救了大约 2600 万人的生命。几十年来，该计划得到了两党的广泛支持。

contentParagraphs.en: But the Trump administration has radically changed the way the U.S. delivers foreign assistance, making sharp cuts and creating uncertainty about future funding. So as my colleagues and I tracked these developments, we wanted to see first-hand what these sweeping changes could mean for the worldwide fight to combat HIV/AIDS.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但特朗普政府从根本上改变了美国提供对外援助的方式，大幅削减了援助金额，并给未来的资金带来了不确定性。因此，当我和我的同事跟踪这些进展时，我们希望亲眼目睹这些彻底的变化对全世界抗击艾滋病毒/艾滋病的斗争意味着什么。

contentParagraphs.en: A notice informs of the ceasing of the CATALYST study in January 2025 due to USA policy changes and funding cuts, as well as alternate options for HIV prevention and healthcare services outside the WITS RHI Women's Health Clinic in Esselen street, Hillbrow, Johannesburg, South Africa on May 25, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一份通知通知，由于美国政策变化和资金削减，以及 2026 年 5 月 25 日在南非约翰内斯堡希尔布罗埃塞伦街 WITS RHI 妇女健康诊所外艾滋病毒预防和医疗保健服务的替代选择，CATALYST 研究将于 2025 年 1 月停止。

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: That curiosity is what led us to Soweto Township in South Africa to sit down with Mazibuko. He's an activist and former journalist, who we met at the restaurant he now runs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 正是这种好奇心促使我们来到南非的索韦托镇与 Mazibuko 坐下来。他是一位活动家和前记者，我们是在他现在经营的餐厅认识的。

contentParagraphs.en: Back in 1999, at a time when the disease was still shrouded in stigma and shame, Mazibuko disclosed his own HIV-positive status in a column in South Africa's biggest newspaper.
contentParagraphs.zh: 早在 1999 年，当这种疾病还笼罩在耻辱和耻辱之中时，马齐布科在南非最大报纸的专栏中披露了自己的艾滋病毒呈阳性状况。

contentParagraphs.en: Even at funerals for those who had died after contracting HIV, Mazibuko told me, "people spoke in hushed voices about what the cause of death could have been, even if they knew."
contentParagraphs.zh: 马兹布科告诉我，即使是在感染艾滋病毒后死亡的人的葬礼上，“人们也会低声谈论死因，即使他们知道。”

contentParagraphs.en: When I asked him why he chose to come forward so publicly, he grew emotional as he told me that he felt he had no other choice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当我问他为什么选择如此公开地站出来时，他变得情绪激动，告诉我他觉得自己别无选择。

contentParagraphs.en: People walk past the closed WITS RHI Women's Health Clinic in Esselen street, Hillbrow, Johannesburg, South Africa on May 25, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 5 月 25 日，人们走过南非约翰内斯堡 Hillbrow 埃塞伦街关闭的 WITS RHI 妇女健康诊所。

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

contentParagraphs.en: Over the course of nearly two weeks of reporting, alongside my All Things Considered colleagues Matt Ozug and Vincent Acovino, we heard stories of how the shifts in foreign aid have destabilized long-effective programs in South Africa and neighboring Mozambique.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在近两周的报道过程中，我们与我的同事马特·奥祖格和文森特·阿科维诺一起，了解了外国援助的变化如何破坏了南非和邻国莫桑比克长期有效项目的稳定。

contentParagraphs.en: South Africa still has the highest number of people with HIV of any country, and the U.S. Embassy in Mozambique notes it is home to the second-largest AIDS epidemic in the world. Our reporting in both countries was supported by the Pulitzer Center.
contentParagraphs.zh: 南非仍然是艾滋病毒感染人数最多的国家，美国驻莫桑比克大使馆指出，南非是世界上第二大艾滋病流行地区。我们在这两个国家的报道得到了普利策中心的支持。

contentParagraphs.en: We spoke with public health workers who worried that the shifts have created uncertainty that could lead to loss of life or more infections. But perhaps what stuck with me the most were the stories of resilience.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我们与公共卫生工作者进行了交谈，他们担心这些变化造成了不确定性，可能导致生命损失或更多感染。但也许最让我印象深刻的是坚韧的故事。

contentParagraphs.en: From the health workers going without a full paycheck to make sure they still have the trust of patients in their communities; to the innovative TV show educating viewers on healthy relationship dynamics; to the advocates doing everything they can offer sex workers personalized care after the closure of a U.S.-funded clinic – everywhere we went, we met people who remained deeply committed to their work.
contentParagraphs.zh: 从没有全额薪水的卫生工作者到确保他们仍然得到社区患者的信任；创新电视节目教育观众健康的关系动态；在美国资助的诊所关闭后，倡导者们竭尽全力为性工作者提供个性化护理——无论我们走到哪里，我们都遇到了仍然坚定致力于工作的人们。

contentParagraphs.en: You can hear our stories here, and see more in the coming days across NPR's social media channels.
contentParagraphs.zh: 您可以在这里听到我们的故事，并在未来几天通过 NPR 的社交媒体渠道看到更多内容。

contentParagraphs.en: Passersby outside the WITS RHI Women's Health Clinic in Esselen street, Hillbrow, Johannesburg, South Africa on May 25, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 5 月 25 日，南非约翰内斯堡 Hillbrow 埃塞伦街 WITS RHI 妇女健康诊所外的路人。

contentParagraphs.en: Gulshan Khan for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的高尔山·汗

articles.title: The gamers taking on the industry to stop it switching off games
articles.summary: Stop Killing Games is challenging the idea publishers can shut down a game without making sure it's still playable.
articles.titleZh: 游戏玩家向行业发起挑战，阻止其关闭游戏
articles.summaryZh: Stop Killing Games 正在挑战发行商可以在不确保游戏仍然可玩的情况下关闭游戏的想法。

contentParagraphs.en: The gamers taking on the industry to stop it switching off games11 hours agoLaura CressTechnology reporterUbisoftThe Crew was released by Ubisoft in 2014, and discontinued in 2024Can a company take away something you've already paid for?In the world of online video games, some already do. Publishers can decide to switch off a game's servers, often leaving it effectively unplayable.Stop Killing Games, a growing consumer rights campaign started by American YouTuber Ross Scott in 2024, is challenging that practice.In January, the group submitted a petition featuring nearly 1.3 million signatures to the European Commission, triggering a public hearing in the European Parliament in April. What began as an online campaign is now awaiting a decision from one of the EU's most powerful institutions.Scott's campaign began following an announcement from the major studio Ubisoft, saying it would shut down the online-only racing game The Crew in 2024.The French company said it was taking the game, which attracted more than 12 million players during its lifetime, offline, citing "upcoming server infrastructure and licensing constraints".For players such as Chemicalflood, who told me he had been playing The Crew for nearly a decade, the move - which left the game unplayable - felt personal."I was around 18 at the time of the launch - it was a big part of my adult life growing up," he said. "It was a great escape from hardship at the time, so it has always been something special to me."Over the years, he said, the game became something he shared with his children, who enjoyed exploring its virtual recreation of the United States."The shutdown itself wasn't upsetting," he explained. "But how they handled it was the kick in the teeth."For Chemicalflood and many fans like him, the issue was not that Ubisoft ended support. It was that players lost access altogether.Stop Killing GamesRoss Scott is the founder of the Stop Killing Games initiative, which he started in 2024The announcement from Ubisoft caught the attention of Scott, also known online as Accursed Farms, who had already been creating content around the issue of ownership around games for several years."I just hate seeing creative works effectively destroyed," he told me.He quickly decided to start a campaign, naming it Stop Killing Games - the killing referring to when "every copy of that game that's ever been sold has been disabled, and no one on the planet can run it".Whammy4, a gamer who founded the fan community The Crew Unlimited and helped lead efforts to preserve the game after its shutdown, likened it to "someone just breaking into your home and stealing your bike or your car"."You buy a physical copy of a game, you bring it home and install the game, you play it for some amount of time. Then all of a sudden the publisher completely destroys all copies of the game worldwide, including yours.""No refunds, no actual heads-up at the time of purchase, and nothing you can do to keep it at all," he said.Industry responseThe lawsuit was dismissed without prejudice in June 2025, after the plaintiffs voluntarily withdrew the case.The wider games industry has also pushed back against the campaign. It also warned that some of the campaign's proposals could make online-only games significantly more expensive to develop."In no way are we asking companies to keep servers running or services going, they can end it any time they want," said Scott.Instead, he and his fellow campaigners argue that when a game is shut down it should be done "responsibly", with publishers considering "end-of-life plans" such as updating the game to work offline or releasing software that allows players to continue running it.Live-service gamesWhile The Crew may have lit the touchpaper for Stop Killing Games' launch, there have been many games before and since which have suddenly been shut down. The issue has become more prominent as online-dependent "live-service" games have grown across the industry. In May, Sony announced plans to discontinue support for the multiplayer title Destruction AllStars.SonyConcord was launched by Sony in August 2024, and taken down just two weeks laterJoost van Dreunen, a professor of games business at NYU Stern, argues that unlike books, films or music, many games are built around communities and online interaction."Games, especially live-service games, are more like digital communities and much less so consumable experiences," he said.But sustaining those communities has become increasingly difficult in a market dominated by long-running successes such as Fortnite and Call of Duty, he explained.As audiences shrink, publishers often decide to shut down servers and move on."Every new live-service game invents its own demise," van Dreunen said.The campaign reaches parliamentThe campaign is now being fought on multiple fronts, and as such features a team of people, including organiser Moritz Katzner, advocating for it alongside Scott.The European Commission must respond to the European Citizens' Initiative - the petition brought by the group - by 27 July.In March, French consumer group UFC-Que Choisir launched legal action against Ubisoft over the shutdown of The Crew, arguing that players were misled about the permanence of their purchase and that some of the company's contract terms were unfair. The case remains ongoing - Ubisoft said it did not comment on ongoing litigation when asked for comment.The UK government has so far resisted calls for new legislation. Although a Stop Killing Games petition secured a parliamentary debate, with over 100,000 signatures, ministers said they had no plans to amend consumer law."Those selling games must comply with existing requirements in consumer law, and we will continue to monitor this issue," they added.Stop Killing GamesThe Stop Killing Games team has grown since the campaign was first launched by Ross Scott in 2024Meanwhile, in the United States, campaigners have backed California's proposed Protect Ou
contentParagraphs.zh: 游戏玩家向业界发起挑战，阻止其关闭游戏 11 小时前劳拉·克雷斯 (Laura Cress) 科技记者育碧《The Crew》由育碧于 2014 年发布，并于 2024 年停产。一家公司可以拿走你已经付费的东西吗？在在线视频游戏领域，有些公司已经这样做了。发行商可以决定关闭游戏的服务器，通常会导致游戏无法玩。美国 YouTuber Ross Scott 在 2024 年发起的一项日益壮大的消费者权益运动“Stop Killing Games”正在挑战这种做法。1 月，该组织向欧盟委员会提交了一份有近 130 万个签名的请愿书，并于 4 月在欧洲议会引发了一场公开听证会。最初是一场在线活动，现在正在等待欧盟最强大机构之一的决定。斯科特的活动是在大型工作室育碧宣布将于 2024 年关闭纯在线赛车游戏《飙酷车神》后开始的。这家法国公司表示，它将把这款在其生命周期内吸引了超过 1200 万玩家的游戏转为离线游戏，理由是“即将到来的服务器基础设施和许可限制”。对于像 Chemicalflood 这样的玩家，他告诉我他一直在玩《飙酷车神》。 Crew 已经工作了近 10 年，这一举动让这款游戏无法玩，让人感觉很个人化。“发布这款游戏时我大约 18 岁，这是我成年生活成长的重要组成部分，”他说。 “这在当时是一次摆脱困境的好机会，所以它对我来说一直是一种特别的东西。”他说，多年来，这款游戏成为他与孩子们分享的东西，他们喜欢探索美国的虚拟娱乐。“关闭本身并不令人不安，”他解释道。 “但他们的处理方式却是令人震惊的。” 对于 Chemicalflood 和许多像他一样的粉丝来说，问题不在于育碧终止了支持。问题是玩家完全失去了访问权限。停止杀戮游戏罗斯·斯科特是“停止杀戮游戏”倡议的创始人，他于 2024 年发起。育碧的公告引起了斯科特的注意，斯科特在网上也被称为“诅咒农场”，多年来他一直在围绕游戏所有权问题创建内容。“我只是讨厌看到创造性的作品被有效地摧毁，”他告诉我。他很快决定发起一项活动，将其命名为“停止杀戮游戏”——杀戮指的是当“每一个副本Whammy4 是一位游戏玩家，他创立了粉丝社区 The Crew Unlimited，并在游戏关闭后帮助领导了保护游戏的工作，他将其比作“有人闯入你的家并偷走了你的自行车或汽车”。“你购买了游戏的实体副本，将其带回家并安装游戏，玩了一段时间，然后发行商突然完全销毁了该游戏的所有副本。他说：“没有退款，购买时没有实际提示，而且你根本无法保留它。”行业反应 在原告自愿撤回诉讼后，该诉讼于 2025 年 6 月在没有偏见的情况下被驳回。更广泛的游戏行业也反对这一活动。它还警告说，该活动的一些建议可能会使开发纯在线游戏的成本显着提高。斯科特说：“我们绝不要求公司保持服务器运行或服务运行，他们可以随时结束它。”相反，他和他的活动人士认为，当游戏被关闭时，应该“负责任地”完成，发行商应考虑“生命周期终止计划”，例如更新游戏以离线运行或发布允许玩家继续运行游戏的软件。 《飙酷车神》可能为《Stop Killing Games》的推出点燃了火炬，在此之前和之后，已经有很多游戏突然被关闭了。随着依赖在线的“实时服务”游戏在整个行业的发展，这个问题变得更加突出。今年 5 月，索尼宣布计划停止支持多人游戏《Destruction AllStars》。SonyConcord 于 2024 年 8 月由索尼推出，仅两周后就被下架。纽约大学斯特恩商学院游戏业务教授 Joost van Dreunen 认为，与书籍、电影或音乐不同，许多游戏都是围绕社区和在线互动构建的。“游戏，尤其是实时服务游戏，更像是数字社区，而不是消费性体验，”他说。他解释说，在《堡垒之夜》和《使命召唤》等长期成功的市场中，社区变得越来越困难。随着受众的减少，发行商常常决定关闭服务器并继续前进。“每一个新的实时服务游戏都会发明自己的灭亡。”范德鲁南说。该运动到达了议会。该运动现在在多个战线上进行，因此有一个团队，包括组织者莫里茨·卡茨纳，与斯科特一起倡导这一运动。欧盟委员会必须对此做出回应欧洲公民倡议组织于 7 月 27 日提出请愿书。 今年 3 月，法国消费者组织 UFC-Que Choisir 就《飙酷车神》的关闭对育碧发起了法律诉讼，称玩家在购买的持久性方面受到了误导，而且该公司的一些合同条款不公平。此案仍在审理中——育碧表示，当被要求置评时，它不会对正在进行的诉讼发表评论。英国政府迄今为止拒绝了新立法的呼吁。尽管“停止杀戮游戏”请愿书获得了议会辩论，并获得了超过 10 万个签名，但部长们表示，他们没有计划修改消费者法。“那些销售游戏的人必须遵守消费者法的现有要求，我们将继续关注这个问题。”他们补充道。“停止杀戮游戏”自罗斯·斯科特于 2024 年首次发起该活动以来，“停止杀戮游戏”团队不断壮大。与此同时，在美国，活动家们支持加州提出的“保护 Ou”计划。

articles.title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. men's final tune-up games before the World Cup
articles.summary: The U.S. men's national team chose to play a pair of highly-ranked, super competitive teams in the final lead-up to the World Cup: Senegal and Germany. The matches showed the U.S. is ready.
articles.titleZh: 世界杯前美国男足最后热身赛的4点收获
articles.summaryZh: 美国男足在世界杯决赛中选择对阵两支排名靠前、竞争力强的球队：塞内加尔和德国。比赛表明美国已经做好准备。

contentParagraphs.en: Players of the United States pose for a team photograph prior to their World Cup tune-up match against Germany at Chicago's Soldier Field on Saturday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周六，美国队球员在芝加哥士兵球场对阵德国队的世界杯热身赛前合影留念。

contentParagraphs.en: It's here, folks: The FIFA World Cup kicks off this week, and the U.S. men's national soccer team is ready for its Friday opener in Los Angeles, the players say.
contentParagraphs.zh: 伙计们，就在这里：国际足联世界杯将于本周拉开帷幕，美国男子国家足球队已准备好迎接周五在洛杉矶举行的揭幕战，球员们说。

contentParagraphs.en: A pair of international friendlies over the past two weekends has given the Americans and their fans plenty of reasons to dream big. Star forward Christian Pulisic broke his monthslong goal drought against Senegal, and defender Antonee Robinson wowed with his offensive playmaking. And above all, the U.S. showed they are unwilling to be intimidated by quality opponents with their own serious aspirations for the World Cup.
contentParagraphs.zh: 过去两个周末的两场国际友谊赛给了美国人和他们的球迷足够的理由去梦想。明星前锋克里斯蒂安·普利西奇在对阵塞内加尔的比赛中打破了长达数月的进球荒，后卫安东尼·罗宾逊的进攻组织能力令人惊叹。最重要的是，美国队表现出他们不愿意被实力强劲的对手吓倒，他们对世界杯有着强烈的渴望。

contentParagraphs.en: "We're really starting to hit our stride," said midfielder Tyler Adams after Saturday's game against Germany.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们真的开始大踏步前进了，”中场球员泰勒·亚当斯在周六对阵德国的比赛后说道。

contentParagraphs.en: Gone are the anxieties about scoring chances
contentParagraphs.zh: 对得分机会的焦虑已经消失

contentParagraphs.en: In the 2022 World Cup, the Americans only managed to score three goals in their four games. That was enough for a win and two draws in the group stage, but their road ended in the Round of 16 when the Netherlands easily outscored them 3-1.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2022年世界杯，美国队在四场比赛中只打进3球。这足以让他们在小组赛中取得一场胜利和两场平局，但他们的道路在16强中被荷兰队以3-1轻松击败他们而结束。

contentParagraphs.en: Now, any anxiety over the U.S. scoring capability feels like a distant memory. The team is flush with options on the attack, and not only Pulisic, who has scored 33 goals for the U.S. in his career. Forwards Folarin Balogun, who found the net against Senegal, and Ricardo Pepi, who was instrumental in two goals against Senegal, have looked excellent these past two weeks.
contentParagraphs.zh: 现在，任何对美国得分能力的焦虑都像是遥远的记忆。这支球队在进攻上有很多选择，不仅仅是普利西奇，他在职业生涯中为美国队打进了33个进球。在对阵塞内加尔队的比赛中进球的前锋弗拉林·巴洛贡和在对阵塞内加尔队的比赛中贡献两粒进球的里卡多·佩皮在过去两周表现出色。

contentParagraphs.en: In other words, the team is consistently creating chances and converting enough to compete. "It's definitely encouraging," said Pulisic Saturday. "We have a lot of talent on the team, a lot of guys that can create and be dangerous to score goals."
contentParagraphs.zh: 换句话说，球队不断创造机会并转化足够的力量来竞争。 “这绝对令人鼓舞，”普利西奇星期六说。 “我们队里有很多才华横溢的人，很多人可以创造机会，也可以制造危险的进球。”

contentParagraphs.en: But defense is still a liability…
contentParagraphs.zh: 但防守仍然是一种负担……

contentParagraphs.en: Both Germany and Senegal picked up easy goals on defensive lapses. Great World Cup teams, like the kind the U.S. hopes to face in the Round of 16 and beyond, will do that.
contentParagraphs.zh: 德国和塞内加尔都因防守失误而轻松进球。伟大的世界杯球队，比如美国队希望在 16 强及以后的比赛中遇到的球队，将会做到这一点。

contentParagraphs.en: Compared to a relatively deep bench of forwards and midfielders, the U.S. have fewer full-package defenders. On one hand, there's Tim Ream, whose soccer IQ and positioning are excellent, but who is 38 and can no longer win a footrace. Next to him is the promising 21-year-old Alex Freeman, the son of a former NFL wide receiver whose athleticism is off the charts but feel for the game is still a work in progress. Backups Miles Robinson, Mark McKenzie and Auston Trusty have their moments but are prone to mistakes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 与相对较深的前锋和中场球员相比，美国队的全能后卫较少。一方面，蒂姆·雷姆（Tim Ream）的足球智商和位置都非常出色，但他已经 38 岁了，无法再赢得一场赛跑。他旁边是前途光明的 21 岁小伙子亚历克斯·弗里曼 (Alex Freeman)，他是前 NFL 外接手的儿子，他的运动能力超乎寻常，但对比赛的感觉仍在进步。替补迈尔斯·罗宾逊（Miles Robinson）、马克·麦肯齐（Mark McKenzie）和奥斯顿·图斯蒂（Auston Trusty）都有自己的表现，但很容易出错。

contentParagraphs.en: "There's been a lot of combinations worked on in training and, there were moments when we can be better connected as a group on the defensive side," Ream said after the game.
contentParagraphs.zh: 雷姆赛后表示：“训练中进行了很多组合，有时我们可以在防守端更好地团结起来。”

contentParagraphs.en: … so getting defender Chris Richards back from injury will be key
contentParagraphs.zh: ……所以让后卫克里斯·理查兹伤愈复出将是关键

contentParagraphs.en: The U.S. badly needs the return of defender Chris Richards, who hurt his ankle in a game with his club Crystal Palace in May. He sat out both friendlies. His status for Friday's game against Paraguay is still in limbo.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国队迫切需要后卫克里斯·理查兹的回归，他在五月份与俱乐部水晶宫的比赛中脚踝受伤。他缺席了两场友谊赛。他在周五对阵巴拉圭的比赛中的状态仍然悬而未决。

contentParagraphs.en: "If this was the final of the World Cup, maybe he can play. But the advice of the medical [team] is not to play," coach Mauricio Pochettino said the day before the Germany game. He added that they would assess Richards' health in the days that followed.
contentParagraphs.zh: “如果这是世界杯决赛，也许他可以参加比赛。但医疗团队的建议是不要参加比赛，”主教练毛里西奥·波切蒂诺在德国队比赛前一天说道。他补充说，他们将在接下来的几天内评估理查兹的健康状况。

contentParagraphs.en: "He's an important piece of the group [with] his energy, his leadership on and off the field. So obviously we're just all behind him and can't wait to have him back," midfielder Weston McKennie said Friday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 中场球员韦斯顿·麦肯尼周五表示：“他是球队的重要一员，充满活力，场内场外都有领导力。所以显然我们都支持他，迫不及待地想让他回来。”

contentParagraphs.en: These guys aren't afraid of adversity
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些人不怕逆境

contentParagraphs.en: A meeker U.S. team might have folded when Germany scored in the second minute of Saturday's game. But this version of the USMNT righted the ship within minutes and began pressing Germany hard, producing chance after chance before finally connecting on Robinson's extraordinary goal before the halftime break.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当德国队在周六比赛的第二分钟进球时，一支更加温和的美国队可能会崩溃。但这个版本的美国国家队在几分钟内就扭转了局面，并开始向德国队施加压力，创造了一个又一个的机会，最后在半场休息前与罗宾逊的非凡进球联系在一起。

contentParagraphs.en: After the game, Pochettino told reporters he came to see Germany's early goal as "lucky" for his squad. "[It was] an amazing challenge for us to see how we react, how is your character, how we show togetherness, how we start to play under pressure," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 赛后，波切蒂诺告诉记者，他认为德国队的早期进球对他的球队来说是“幸运的”。 “这对我们来说是一个惊人的挑战，看看我们如何反应，你的性格如何，我们如何表现出团结，我们如何在压力下开始比赛，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: And the toughness showed up in the physicality, too. Players didn't back down from challenges. When Germany fouled hard, an American delivered a hard foul right back. The message, Adams said, was "have each other's backs."
contentParagraphs.zh: 坚韧也体现在身体上。球员们没有在挑战面前退缩。当德国队犯规时，美国队右后卫犯规。亚当斯说，传达的信息是“互相支持”。

contentParagraphs.en: "We can tune up passing, final plays, finishing, all those kinds of things. But to see that mentality, I think from everyone, and it's not just the guys that started, everyone that came off the bench as well — that's what you need," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们可以调整传球、最后的表现、终结，所有这些事情。但是要看到这种心态，我认为从每个人身上，不仅仅是首发球员，还有替补出场的每个人——这就是你所需要的，”他说。

articles.title: Kosovo votes again amid political deadlock, seeking EU and NATO progress
articles.summary: Deadlock hampers Kosovo’s EU ambitions as voters head to polls amid economic struggles and political discord.
articles.titleZh: 科索沃在政治僵局中再次投票，寻求欧盟和北约进展
articles.summaryZh: 由于选民在经济困难和政治不和的情况下前往投票，僵局阻碍了科索沃的欧盟雄心。

contentParagraphs.en: Prolonged crisis has negatively affected Kosovo’s economy, already hit hard by the global energy crisis and rising fuel prices.Voters in Kosovo headed to the polls for an early parliamentary election, the third in 18 months, in an attempt to unlock a political impasse in the small Balkan nation that is striving to join the European Union and NATO.Sunday’s ballot was scheduled after the main political parties failed to agree by a March deadline on who should replace former President Vjosa Osmani. The first inconclusive election in February 2025 left the country without a functioning government for much of last year, forcing a second election in December.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Who is Roelf Meyer, South Africa’s new ambassador to the US?list 2 of 3Kosovo court jails three Serb separatists over 2023 Banjska attacklist 3 of 3Serbia hosts first joint military exercise with NATOend of listThe prolonged crisis has negatively affected Kosovo’s economy, already hit hard by the global energy crisis and rising fuel prices. One of the youngest and poorest countries in Europe, it declared independence from Serbia in 2008 after a 1998-99 war, which ended in a NATO bombing campaign that forced Serbia to withdraw.Prime Minister Albin Kurti’s centre-left Vetevendosje party has held a clear parliamentary majority since the early election in December. But Kosovo’s president is elected by at least 80 lawmakers in the 120-member assembly, requiring a broader political consensus.Kurti is being challenged by the two main opposition parties, the Democratic Party of Kosovo and the Democratic League of Kosovo, which have accused him of seeking to impose full control over all political institutions in the country.Former President Osmani is running on the opposition LDK list in the election, having turned against Kurti after he refused to back her for a second term in office.While the key players have blamed each other for the crisis, their inability to reach a compromise has led to disappointment among Kosovo’s about two million voters, who want the government to focus on the economy and living standards instead.Analysts still do not expect major changes in the election outcome compared to the previous vote in December.The institutional vacuum, without a stable government, has delayed access to the EU and other international funds available to the country.European Council President Antonio Costa urged Kosovo during a visit last week to end the political impasse and unite over the goal of EU integration.Kosovo has been recognised by the United States and most EU countries, but not by Serbia and its allies, Russia and China.Tensions remain high in Kosovo’s north, where most of the ethnic Serbian minority live, but both Pristina and Belgrade have been told they must mend relations to move forward with their EU membership bids.
contentParagraphs.zh: 科索沃的经济已经受到全球能源危机和燃油价格上涨的严重打击，旷日持久的危机对科索沃经济产生了负面影响。科索沃选民前往投票站提前举行议会选举，这是 18 个月内的第三次选举，试图打破这个正在努力加入欧盟和北约的巴尔干小国的政治僵局。主要政党未能在 3 月份的最后期限前就谁应接替前总统维约萨·奥斯马尼达成一致，因此定于周日进行投票。 2025 年 2 月的第一次选举没有结果，导致该国在去年的大部分时间里政府都没有正常运作，迫使该国在 12 月举行第二次选举。 推荐故事列表 3 项列表 1 of 3 谁是南非新驻美国大使罗尔夫·迈耶 (Roelf Meyer)？列表 2 (共 3) 科索沃法院因 2023 年巴尼斯卡袭击而监禁了三名塞尔维亚分裂分子列表 3 (共 3) 塞尔维亚与北约举办了首次联合军事演习持续的危机对科索沃经济产生了负面影响，该国经济已经受到全球能源危机和燃油价格上涨的严重打击。作为欧洲最年轻和最贫穷的国家之一，塞尔维亚在 1998-99 年战争之后于 2008 年宣布独立，这场战争以北约轰炸行动结束，迫使塞尔维亚撤军。自 12 月提前选举以来，总理阿尔宾·库尔蒂 (Albin Kurti) 领导的中左翼维特文多斯耶党 (Vetevendosje) 一直在议会中占据明显多数席位。但科索沃总统是由 120 名议员中至少 80 名议员选举产生的，需要更广泛的政治共识。库尔蒂受到科索沃民主党和科索沃民主联盟这两个主要反对党的挑战，它们指责他寻求全面控制该国所有政治机构。前总统奥斯马尼在选举中被列入反对党民主民主党名单，他在拒绝支持库尔蒂连任后转而反对库尔蒂。虽然主要参与者相互指责对方造成了危机，但他们无法达成妥协，导致科索沃约 200 万选民感到失望，他们希望政府将重点放在经济和生活水平上。分析人士仍然预计，与 12 月份的上次投票相比，选举结果不会发生重大变化。由于没有稳定的政府，机构真空推迟了该国获得欧盟和其他国际资金的机会。欧洲理事会主席安东尼奥·科斯塔在上周访问期间敦促科索沃结束政治僵局，团结一致实现欧盟一体化的目标。科索沃得到了美国和大多数欧盟国家的承认，但没有得到塞尔维亚及其盟国俄罗斯和中国的承认。科索沃北部的紧张局势仍然高度紧张，那里居住着大多数塞尔维亚少数民族，但普里什蒂纳和贝尔格莱德都被告知必须修补关系，以推进加入欧盟的努力。

articles.title: How have countries around the world responded to the US-Israel war on Iran?
articles.summary: The war, which has entered its 100th day, has triggered global energy crisis, drawing concerns from world leaders.
articles.titleZh: 世界各国如何应对美以对伊朗的战争？
articles.summaryZh: 这场战争已进入第100天，引发了全球能源危机，引起世界领导人的担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: Sunday marks 100 days since the United States and Israel launched war on Iran – a conflict that has triggered a global energy crisis and darkened the global economic outlook.The war, which Iran has called an “unprovoked act of aggression”, has expanded to Gulf nations as well as Lebanon. A fragile ceasefire has been in place since April 8, but Israel has continued its offensive in Lebanon, killing more than 3,000 people.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Iran reinstates some internet access but restrictions remain for mostlist 2 of 3Iran ⁠restores some gas production at South Pars facilitylist 3 of 3US Congress moves to deepen military ties with Israel: Why it mattersend of listAmerica’s European allies did not condemn the US-Israeli attacks, but declined to get involved in the war and expressed their opposition to regime change. The Gulf countries also condemned the Iranian attacks on their territories.Russia and China, the world’s second-largest economy, also pushed back against the war.Countries around the world affected by rising oil prices and market volatility have called for a diplomatic solution to the conflict, with Pakistan playing a major role in mediating talks.On Wednesday, Israel and Lebanon renewed the ceasefire agreed on April 16. But that has not stopped Israel from carrying out its attacks on Lebanon, which Tehran has said are a violation of the April 8 ceasefire between Washington and New Delhi.As diplomacy to negotiate a deal between the warring parties drags on, we break down how the rest of the world initially reacted to the war, how affected countries’ positions have evolved, and where they stand now.(Al Jazeera)The Gulf regionGulf states have been caught up in the war since it began on February 28, with Iran launching missile and drone strikes against US military assets hosted on their soil. But Gulf countries have accused Iran of also targeting civilian sites, including airports and energy facilities.Hundreds of days into the war, sporadic attacks continue. Here’s how some countries have been reacting.Oman – As the main mediator of the US-Iran nuclear talks, Oman initially expressed dismay when the war was launched while nuclear talks were under way. Foreign Minister Badr Albusaidi said the conflict would not serve US interests, nor the interests of global peace. Unlike other US allies in the Gulf, such as Qatar, Bahrain and the UAE, Oman does not host US forces.It was nevertheless dragged into the conflict when Iran launched a flurry of retaliatory attacks on US military assets and energy infrastructure across the Gulf region. The Duqm commercial port, located in Al Wusta governorate in central Oman, was struck by two drones on March 1. A fuel tank at the port was also hit in a drone attack two days later. The Salalah port in the country’s west was hit at least twice by drones. Two foreigners were killed in a drone attack in Sohar province on March 13.Iran, which has friendly ties with Oman, has denied it was behind the attacks.In a March 18 article for the Economist, Albusaidi said the US has “lost control of its own foreign policy” and accused Israel of persuading US President Donald Trump’s administration to go to war with Iran. He also said the conflict was a “catastrophe” and a “grave miscalculation”. Last month, Trump threatened Oman with military force if it got involved in the dispute over access to the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route that Iran has choked off during the conflict.Qatar – When the war began, Qatar strongly condemned Iran for firing missiles targeting its territory, which is home to the Al Udeid airbase that hosts US troops. Qatar expelled several Iranian military and diplomatic personnel from the country following an attack on QatarEnergy’s liquefied natural gas (LNG) facility at Ras Laffan.A long-range US AN/FPS-132 missile early-warning radar in Qatar was also damaged by Iranian missiles.Qatar has called for de-escalation and dialogue to end the war. In a phone call, Qatar’s Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani told Trump of the need to prioritise political and diplomatic solutions and dialogue among all parties to consolidate regional security and stability and spare the region further tension and escalation.Trump expressed his appreciation for the role played by the State of Qatar in supporting Pakistan’s mediation efforts and strengthening channels of communication among the various parties.Doha has been in communication with Iranian officials. Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and its main negotiator, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, visited Qatar in May as part of diplomatic efforts to end the conflict.United Arab Emirates (UAE) – When the war began, the UAE’s Ministry of Defence condemned in the “strongest terms” Iran’s attacks on its territory, several of which it said its air defences intercepted. It called the attack “a dangerous escalation and a cowardly act that threatens the security and safety of civilians”, stressing that the UAE has the “full right” to respond.According to a May 29 report by the Wall Street Journal, the UAE also carried out dozens of air strikes against Iran during the Israeli-US war on Iran. The strikes were reportedly coordinated with the US and Israel, which provided the UAE with intelligence. The UAE has also cracked down on Iranians and Iranian businesses based in the country.Iranian authorities have, in turn, increasingly singled out the UAE in their war messaging, and have warned of stronger strikes against the country if the US and Israel resume their attacks. Among Gulf states, the UAE and Kuwait have thus far borne the brunt of Iranian attacks.Smoke rises from an Israeli airstrike that hit Burj al-Shamali village near the southern port city of Tyre in Lebanon on June 2, 2026 [Mohammed Zaatari/AP Photo]Bahrain – When the war began, Bahrain, which hosts the United States Navy’s Fifth Fleet, called attacks on its territory “treacherous”. contentParagraphs.zh: 周日是美国和以色列对伊朗发动战争 100 天，这场冲突引发了全球能源危机，并使全球经济前景黯淡。伊朗称这场战争为“无端侵略行为”，战争已扩大到海湾国家和黎巴嫩。自 4 月 8 日以来，脆弱的停火已经到位，但以色列继续在黎巴嫩发动攻势，造成 3,000 多人死亡。 推荐故事列表 3 项列表 3 项中的 1 项伊朗恢复了部分互联网接入，但对大多数人的限制仍然存在列表 2 项（共 3 项）伊朗恢复了南帕尔斯设施的部分天然气生产列表 3 项中的美国国会采取行动加深与以色列的军事关系：为什么这很重要美国的欧洲盟友没有谴责反对美以袭击，但拒绝卷入战争并表示反对政权更迭。海湾国家也谴责伊朗对其领土的袭击。世界第二大经济体俄罗斯和中国也对这场战争进行了反击。世界各地受到油价上涨和市场波动影响的国家呼吁通过外交手段解决冲突，巴基斯坦在调解谈判中发挥着重要作用。周三，以色列和黎巴嫩续签了4月16日达成的停火协议。但这并没有阻止以色列对黎巴嫩发动袭击，德黑兰称此举违反了4月协议。华盛顿和新德里之间的停火将于 8 月 8 日进行。随着交战各方之间谈判协议的外交谈判持续进行，我们详细分析了世界其他国家最初对这场战争的反应、受影响国家的立场如何演变以及它们现在所处的位置。（半岛电视台）海湾地区自 2 月 28 日战争爆发以来，海湾国家一直深陷战争之中，伊朗对驻扎在其领土上的美国军事资产发动了导弹和无人机袭击。但海湾国家指责伊朗也针对民用场所，包括机场和能源设施。战争已持续数百天，零星袭击仍在继续。以下是一些国家的反应。 阿曼——作为美伊核谈判的主要调解人，阿曼最初对核谈判正在进行期间发动战争表示沮丧。外交部长巴德尔·阿布赛迪表示，这场冲突不符合美国的利益，也不符合全球和平的利益。与卡塔尔、巴林和阿联酋等美国在海湾地区的盟友不同，阿曼没有美军驻扎。然而，当伊朗对海湾地区的美国军事资产和能源基础设施发动一系列报复性袭击时，阿曼被拖入冲突。 3月1日，位于阿曼中部乌斯塔省的杜库姆商业港口遭到两架无人机袭击。两天后，该港口的一个油箱也在无人机袭击中被击中。该国西部的塞拉莱港至少两次遭到无人机袭击。 3月13日，苏哈尔省发生无人机袭击，造成两名外国人死亡。与阿曼关系友好的伊朗否认是此次袭击的幕后黑手。阿尔布赛迪在3月18日为《经济学人》发表的文章中表示，美国“失去了对自己外交政策的控制”，并指责以色列说服美国总统特朗普政府与伊朗开战。他还表示，这场冲突是一场“灾难”和“严重误判”。上个月，特朗普威胁阿曼，如果阿曼卷入霍尔木兹海峡的争端，将动用武力。 卡塔尔——战争开始时，卡塔尔强烈谴责伊朗向其领土发射导弹，该领土是驻扎美军的乌代德空军基地所在地。卡塔尔能源公司位于拉斯拉凡的液化天然气（LNG）设施遭到袭击后，卡塔尔将数名伊朗军事和外交人员驱逐出境。位于卡塔尔的美国远程AN/FPS-132导弹预警雷达也被伊朗导弹损坏。卡塔尔呼吁缓和局势并通过对话结束战争。卡塔尔埃米尔塔米姆·本·哈马德·阿勒萨尼在电话中告诉特朗普，需要优先考虑政治和外交解决方案以及各方之间的对话，以巩固地区安全稳定，避免地区局势进一步紧张和升级。特朗普对卡塔尔国在支持巴基斯坦斡旋努力和加强各方沟通渠道方面发挥的作用表示赞赏。多哈方面一直在与伊朗官员保持沟通。伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格奇及其主要谈判代表、议会议长穆罕默德·巴格尔·加利巴夫于 5 月访问了卡塔尔，作为结束冲突的外交努力的一部分。 阿拉伯联合酋长国（阿联酋）——战争开始时，阿联酋国防部以“最强烈的措辞”谴责伊朗对其领土的袭击，并表示其防空系统拦截了其中几次袭击。其称此次袭击是“危险的升级和威胁平民安全的懦夫行为”，并强调阿联酋拥有“完全权利”做出回应。 据《华尔街日报》5月29日报道，在以美对伊战争期间，阿联酋还对伊朗进行了数十次空袭。据报道，这次袭击是与美国和以色列协调的，美国和以色列向阿联酋提供了情报。阿联酋还对设在该国的伊朗人和伊朗企业进行打击。反过来，伊朗当局在其战争信息中越来越多地针对阿联酋，并警告说，如果美国和以色列恢复袭击，将对该国进行更强有力的打击。在海湾国家中，阿联酋和科威特迄今为止首当其冲地遭受了伊朗的袭击。 2026 年 6 月 2 日，以色列空袭黎巴嫩南部港口城市提尔附近的 Burj al-Shamali 村，升起浓烟[Mohammed Zaatari/美联社照片]巴林 — 战争开始时，美国海军第五舰队所在的巴林称对其领土的袭击是“危险的”。自此以来，我们一直积极地

articles.title: 100 days of the US–Israel war on Iran
articles.summary: One hundred days in, the US–Israel–Iran conflict remains a bloody stalemate with mounting loss of civilian lives.
articles.summaryZh: 一百天过去了，美国、以色列、伊朗冲突仍然陷入血腥僵局，平民伤亡不断增加。

contentParagraphs.en: One hundred days in, the US-Israel-Iran conflict remains a bloody stalemate with mounting loss of civilian lives.Smoke rises over Tehran after an explosion, Iran, 28 February 2026. [AP Photo]Published On 7 Jun 2026One hundred days into the US-Israeli war on Iran, the conflict has settled into a grinding, unpredictable stalemate — far removed from the “very fast” campaign Donald Trump once promised.A Pakistan-brokered ceasefire announced on April 8 has done little to stop the bloodshed.The Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed to maritime traffic, peace talks hover near collapse, and attacks persist across multiple front lines.Israel has expanded the war on Iran to Lebanon, inflicting on it the heavier death toll, and displacing more than one million people as Israel expands its occupation of the country’s south, razing entire villages.Israel killed at least 3,593 people in Lebanon, while the US and Israel killed at least 3,468 in Iran.Some 29 people have died in Gulf countries, as well as 26 in Israel and 13 US soldiers, in Iranian attacks.This photo gallery documents 100 days of war across Iran, Lebanon, and the broader region — from devastated neighbourhoods and crowded funerals to missile-struck airports, gridlocked escape routes, and staged rallies — bearing witness to the human cost of a conflict with no end in sight.This screen grab from handout video shows a missile being launched from a US Navy ship in support of Operation Epic Fury, 28 February 2026. [Vahid Salemi/AP Photo]Mohamed El Junayd, 43, who says he survived the Israeli strike, sits beside piled-up damaged cars at the site of an 8 April Israeli attack on Corniche al-Mazraa in Beirut, Lebanon, 13 April 2026. contentParagraphs.zh: 一百天后，美国-以色列-伊朗冲突仍然陷入血腥僵局，平民生命损失不断增加。2026年2月28日，伊朗爆炸后德黑兰上空升起浓烟。[美联社照片]发布于2026年6月7日美以对伊朗战争一百天后，冲突陷入了令人痛苦的、不可预测的僵局——与唐纳德·特朗普曾经承诺的“非常快”的竞选活动相去甚远。巴基斯坦斡旋于 4 月 8 日宣布的停火并没有起到制止流血的作用。霍尔木兹海峡仍然基本上禁止海上交通，和平谈判几近崩溃，多条前线的袭击持续存在。以色列已将对伊朗的战争扩大到黎巴嫩，造成更严重的死亡人数，并导致超过一百万人流离失所，因为以色列扩大了对该国南部的占领，夷平了整个村庄。以色列杀死了至少 3,593 人黎巴嫩人民被杀，而美国和以色列在伊朗造成至少 3,468 人死亡。在伊朗袭击中，海湾国家约有 29 人死亡，以色列有 26 人死亡，还有 13 名美国士兵死亡。这张照片库记录了伊朗、黎巴嫩和更广泛地区的 100 天战争——从满目疮痍的社区和拥挤的葬礼到被导弹袭击的机场、堵塞的逃生路线和举行的集会——见证了与伊朗的冲突造成的人员伤亡。没有尽头。这张讲义视频的屏幕截图显示，2026 年 2 月 28 日，一艘美国海军舰艇正在发射一枚导弹，以支持史诗般的愤怒行动。[美联社报道的美国中央司令部]2026 年 2 月 28 日，以色列特拉维夫，一栋大楼被伊朗导弹直接击中后，消防队员正在努力扑灭大楼内的火焰。[Tomer Neuberg/美联社照片]一缕黑烟从以色列沙迦市工业区的仓库中升起。阿拉伯联合酋长国报道伊朗于 2026 年 3 月 1 日在迪拜发动袭击。[Altaf Qadri/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 2 日，在伊朗德黑兰举行的美以军事行动期间，以色列和美国的袭击击中了国家电视通讯塔和街对面的附近建筑物，甘地医院的一个房间里堆满了碎片。[Vahid Salemi/美联社照片]在为 165 名女学生举行的集体葬礼上，为受害者准备了坟墓2 月 28 日，伊朗称美国和以色列于 2026 年 3 月 2 日袭击了南部城市米纳布的一所女子学校。[伊朗外国媒体部/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 4 日，伊朗德黑兰，一名神职人员带领志愿者在美以军事行动中袭击的警察设施旁祈祷。[Vahid Salemi/美联社照片]以色列坦克在以色列和黎巴嫩边境附近机动。以色列北部，2026 年 3 月 4 日。[Ariel Schalit/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 4 日，一名牧童从一枚未爆炸的弹丸旁走开，该弹丸落在叙利亚东部卡米什利郊区的一片空地上。[Baderkhan Ahmad/美联社照片]工人们在检查纳巴蒂耶一栋在以色列空袭中被毁的建筑时，看到已故真主党军事指挥官伊马德·穆格尼耶 (Imad Mughniyeh) 的巨幅海报，黎巴嫩南部，2026 年 3 月 5 日。[Mohammed Zaatari/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 8 日，伊朗红新月会的两名妇女站在伊朗德黑兰，美国和以色列对石油储存设施的袭击造成的浓烟升上天空。[Vahid Salemi/美联社照片]人们在检查以色列空袭袭击黎巴嫩南部 Sir al-Gharbiyeh 村几栋房屋的现场， 2026 年 3 月 8 日。[Mohammed Zaatari/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 9 日，以色列对贝鲁特南部郊区 Dahiyeh 进行空袭后，火焰升腾。[Hassan Ammar/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 11 日，伊朗德黑兰，哀悼者参加为在美以军事行动中丧生的伊朗高级军事官员和平民举行的葬礼游行。[Vahid Salemi/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 12 日，伊朗德黑兰，一名妇女坐在美以空袭期间受损的住宅楼对面的废墟上。[Vahid Salemi/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 13 日，伊朗德黑兰南部发生罢工后，救援人员在废墟中寻找幸存者。[Sajjad Safari/美联社照片]以色列空袭靠近德黑兰的 Qasmiyeh 大桥，冒出浓烟和火焰。黎巴嫩南部沿海城市提尔，2026 年 3 月 22 日。[Mohammad Zaatari/美联社照片]2026 年 3 月 28 日，黎巴嫩南部杰津，一名记者携带被烧毁的安全装备。 [Mohammed Zaatari/美联社照片]2026 年 4 月 6 日，以色列海法一栋住宅楼被伊朗导弹击中后的第二天，救援人员和士兵从废墟中抬出受害者的尸体。[Ariel Schalit/美联社照片]2026 年 4 月 8 日，救援人员在黎巴嫩贝鲁特以色列袭击现场工作。[Mohamed Azakir/路透社]哀悼者参加尸体接收仪式2026 年 4 月 11 日，在黎巴嫩南部西顿，以色列对纳巴蒂耶的袭击中丧生的黎巴嫩国家安全部队成员。[阿齐兹·塔赫尔/路透社]2026 年 4 月 11 日，在黎巴嫩贝鲁特，一名示威者在抗议以色列和黎巴嫩之间的直接谈判时亲吻了象征性的靴子，向一名黎巴嫩战士致敬。[阿德南·阿比迪/路透社]一名男子在黎巴嫩贝鲁特举着伊朗国旗，打出胜利的手势。 2026 年 4 月 13 日，伊朗德黑兰市中心伊斯兰革命广场，一块反美广告牌前，广告牌上显示美国飞机被伊朗军队的渔网困住，广告牌上写着波斯语标语，上面写着“霍尔木兹海峡将保持关闭”和“整个波斯湾都是我们的猎场”。[Vahid Salemi/美联社照片]43 岁的穆罕默德·朱奈德 (Mohamed El Junayd) 坐在堆积如山的受损汽车旁边，他说自己在以色列袭击中幸存下来。 2026 年 4 月 13 articles.title: How the hosts are preparing for an Ebola outbreak during World Cup 2026
articles.summary: The US, Mexico and Canada have announced Ebola-related travel restrictions before the start of the World Cup.
articles.titleZh: 东道主如何应对 2026 年世界杯期间埃博拉疫情的爆发
articles.summaryZh: 美国、墨西哥和加拿大在世界杯开始前宣布了与埃博拉病毒相关的旅行限制。

contentParagraphs.en: Fans from around the world are starting to arrive in North America for the largest-ever World Cup, but an outbreak of the Ebola virus in East Africa has put health officials in the host nations on high alert.Authorities are racing to contain the outbreak of the rare Bundibugyo strain of the virus, which was first declared in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) on May 15. It has since infected at least 488 people there, causing 86 deaths.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Women bear the brunt of DRC’s Ebola outbreaklist 2 of 4Spain bans DR Congo World Cup warm-up due to Ebola fearslist 3 of 4The Ebola outbreak the world isn’t paying attention tolist 4 of 4What are the Ebola vaccines under research, and when will they be ready?end of listThe disease has also spread to neighbouring Uganda, and the World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the outbreak a “public health emergency of international concern”.Uganda has confirmed 19 cases and two deaths and has largely closed off its western border with the DRC in an effort to curb cross-border contagion, frustrating traders who rely on border crossings for business.The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has warned that the current outbreak could swell to become the largest Ebola epidemic on record, rivalling the 2014-16 epidemic in West Africa.With more than a million fans expected to travel to North America for the World Cup, here’s how authorities in the United States, Canada and Mexico are preparing to stop the spread of Ebola during the tournament.Travel restrictions and airport screeningThe World Cup host nations announced “aligned public health travel measures for individuals coming from African regions at greatest risk from the Ebola virus”, according to a joint statement from the three countries, which did not provide details of the aligned measures.In May, the US banned all noncitizens who had travelled to the DRC, Uganda or South Sudan in the previous 21 days from entering the country. This ban was then extended to green card holders who were in those countries in the previous 21 days.A number of US airports, including Washington Dulles and Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta, have also implemented enhanced screening measures for travellers from affected regions.Canada has temporarily banned residents of the DRC, Uganda and South Sudan from entering the country for ⁠90 days, according to the country’s public health agency.Canadian citizens, permanent residents and other foreign nationals who have been in affected areas in recent weeks and do not have symptoms will ⁠have to quarantine for 21 days, with the new rule kicking in from May 30.Mexico’s health secretary has outlined ⁠tighter Ebola screening measures at airports, urging the public to avoid travel to the DRC and asking arrivals from the country to observe a 21-day quarantine.DRC players and staff have been isolating in Belgium before heading to the US [Nicolas Economou/Reuters]DRC forced to isolate in BelgiumThe DRC, who have qualified for their first World Cup since 1974, cancelled a planned pre-World Cup training camp at home due to the Ebola outbreak and have been based in Belgium instead.Andrew Giuliani, executive director of the White House Task Force for the World Cup, confirmed to ESPN on May 23 that the Congolese delegation must maintain a bubble in Belgium and isolate for 21 days or risk being denied entry to the US.The team’s World Cup preparations were further thrown into chaos when a planned warm-up match against Chile in Spain was cancelled due to fears over the spread of the virus.“I have signed the decree banning the holding of the June 9 match between the Democratic Republic of Congo and Chile,” said Juan Franco, mayor of La Linea de la Concepcion in southern Spain.DRC coach Sebastien Desabre has offered to play the match behind closed doors instead.“The only thing I can say is that we’re used to adapting, and whatever happens, we’ll have no problem adapting to all these situations,” he added.During the tournament, the East African team plan to be based in Houston, Texas, where they will play their first Group K match on June 17 against Portugal.The DRC are then scheduled to play Colombia on June 24 in Guadalajara, Mexico, before their last group match against Uzbekistan in Atlanta, Georgia, US, on June 28.Tracking outbreaksBoston University’s Center on Emerging Infectious Diseases will be monitoring the World Cup to track any outbreaks of infectious diseases.Their Biothreats Emergence Analysis and Communications Network (BEACON) programme aims to “keep public health officials, participants, and the global community informed, safe, and one step ahead”, according to an update on their website.The National Special Pathogen System (NSPS) also recently conducted a tabletop exercise, simulating responses to any disease outbreaks during the tournament.Their mock scenario focused on an outbreak of Middle East Respiratory Syndrome during the World Cup.The exercise highlighted the “importance of timely actions, coordinated efforts, and comprehensive planning”, according to NSPS.‘Very low risk’ to fansDespite these measures, health experts are not overly concerned about the risks facing fans who are travelling to North America this summer.“If you are a casual visitor to the World Cup from around the world, I think there is a very low risk that you would be at risk of Ebola,” Oliver Johnson, a global health academic at King’s College London, told the Reuters news agency.“Ebola has never really caused transmission in high-income country settings, where there have been very occasional cases.“That is because it is not airborne. You had to normally have direct contact with someone who is quite sick, and there ‌is ⁠usually good contact tracing. If a case does occur, it is identified quickly,” he added.Fans travelling for the tournament should follow basic precautions such as good hand hygiene and ⁠avoiding close contact if unwell, Johnson said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 来自世界各地的球迷开始抵达北美，观看有史以来规模最大的世界杯，但东非埃博拉病毒的爆发使东道国的卫生官员处于高度戒备状态。当局正在加紧控制罕见的本迪布焦病毒株的爆发，该病毒于 5 月 15 日在刚果民主共和国 (DRC) 首次宣布。此后，该病毒已感染该国至少 488 人，造成 86 人死亡。推荐故事列表 4 项列表1 of 4女性首当其冲遭遇刚果民主共和国埃博拉疫情list 2 of 4西班牙因埃博拉恐惧而禁止刚果民主共和国世界杯热身list 3 of 4世界没有关注埃博拉疫情list 4 of 4正在研究的埃博拉疫苗是什么，什么时候能准备好？列表结束该疾病还传播到邻国乌干达，世界卫生组织（WHO）已宣布该疫情为埃博拉疫情“国际关注的突发公共卫生事件”。乌干达已确诊 19 例病例和 2 例死亡，并已基本关闭了与刚果民主共和国的西部边境，以遏制跨境传染，这让依赖过境点开展业务的商人感到沮丧。美国疾病控制与预防中心 (CDC) 警告称，当前疫情可能会扩大，成为有记录以来最大规模的埃博拉疫情，与 2014-16 年西非疫情相媲美。预计将有超过 100 万粉丝前往乌干达北美世界杯期间，美国、加拿大和墨西哥当局准备如何在比赛期间阻止埃博拉病毒的传播。 旅行限制和机场筛查 根据这三个国家的联合声明，世界杯主办国宣布“针对来自埃博拉病毒风险最大的非洲地区的个人采取统一的公共卫生旅行措施”，但该声明没有提供统一措施的细节。 今年 5 月，美国禁止所有曾前往刚果民主共和国、乌干达或南苏丹的非公民。入境前 21 天。此禁令随后扩大到过去 21 天内到过这些国家的绿卡持有者。包括华盛顿杜勒斯机场和亚特兰大哈茨菲尔德-杰克逊机场在内的多个美国机场也对来自受影响地区的旅客实施了加强筛查措施。据加拿大公共卫生机构称，加拿大已暂时禁止刚果民主共和国、乌干达和南苏丹居民入境 90 天内。最近几周到过受影响地区的加拿大公民、永久居民和其他外国人新规定从 5 月 30 日起生效，没有症状的人必须隔离 21 天。墨西哥卫生部长概述了在机场采取更严格的埃博拉筛查措施，敦促公众避免前往刚果民主共和国，并要求从该国入境的人接受 21 天的隔离。刚果民主共和国的球员和工作人员在前往美国之前一直在比利时隔离 [Nicolas Economou/Reuters]刚果民主共和国被迫在比利时隔离刚果民主共和国，自1974年以来首次获得世界杯参赛资格的刚果队，因埃博拉疫情取消了原定在国内进行的世界杯前集训，转而驻扎比利时。白宫世界杯工作组执行主任安德鲁·朱利安尼5月23日向ESPN证实，刚果代表团必须在比利时保持隔离状态，并隔离21天，否则将面临被拒绝入境美国的风险。原定的热身赛让球队的世界杯备战进一步陷入混乱。由于担心病毒传播，西班牙对阵智利的比赛被取消。西班牙南部拉利内亚德拉康塞普西翁市市长胡安·弗朗哥表示：“我已经签署了禁止举行 6 月 9 日刚果民主共和国与智利之间比赛的法令。”刚果民主共和国主教练塞巴斯蒂安·德萨布雷提出改为空场进行比赛。“我唯一能说的是，我们已经习惯了适应，无论发生什么，我们都会毫无问题地适应。”他补充说：“比赛期间，东非队计划以德克萨斯州休斯顿为基地，他们将于 6 月 17 日在那里进行 K 组的第一场比赛，对阵葡萄牙。刚果民主共和国随后计划于 6 月 24 日在墨西哥瓜达拉哈拉对阵哥伦比亚，最后一场小组赛将于 6 月 28 日在美国佐治亚州亚特兰大对阵乌兹别克斯坦。追踪疫情跟踪任何传染病的爆发。根据其网站上的更新，他们的生物威胁突发分析和通信网络（BEACON）计划旨在“让公共卫生官员、参与者和国际社会了解情况、安全并领先一步”。国家特殊病原体系统（NSPS）最近还进行了一项桌面演习，模拟对比赛期间任何疾病爆发的反应。他们的模拟场景重点关注世界杯期间中东呼吸综合症的爆发。该演习强调了NSPS 表示，“及时采取行动、协调努力和全面规划的重要性”。尽管采取了这些措施，但健康专家并不过分担心今年夏天前往北美旅行的球迷所面临的风险。“如果你是来自世界各地的世界杯临时观众，我认为你感染埃博拉病毒的风险非常低，”伦敦国王学院的全球健康学者奥利弗·约翰逊告诉路透社。确实在高收入国家环境中引起了传播，那里曾出现过非常偶然的病例。“那是因为它不是通过空气传播的。你通常必须与病得很重的人直接接触，而且通常有很好的接触者追踪。如果确实发生了病例，我们会很快发现。”他补充道。约翰逊说，前往观看比赛的球迷应遵循基本的预防措施，例如保持良好的手部卫生，并在身体不适时避免密切接触。

articles.title: Bumblebees have tiny brains but they can solve problems like chimps and elephants
articles.summary: New research suggests the fuzzy insects may be capable of spontaneously solving problems the way animals with much larger brains do.
articles.titleZh: 大黄蜂的大脑很小，但它们可以像黑猩猩和大象一样解决问题
articles.summaryZh: 新的研究表明，这些毛茸茸的昆虫可能能够像大脑更大的动物一样自发地解决问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Bumblebees figured out how to get to an out-of-reach reward in a new study, proving they can problem-solve on the fly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在一项新的研究中，大黄蜂找到了如何获得遥不可及的奖励，证明它们可以即时解决问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Mikko Törmänen/University of Oulu
contentParagraphs.zh: Mikko Törmänen/奥卢大学

contentParagraphs.en: Mikko Törmänen/University of Oulu
contentParagraphs.zh: Mikko Törmänen/奥卢大学

contentParagraphs.en: For new discoveries, everyday mysteries, and the science behind the headlines, follow NPR's ShortWave podcast .
contentParagraphs.zh: 如需了解新发现、日常奥秘以及头条新闻背后的科学，请关注 NPR 的 ShortWave 播客。

contentParagraphs.en: Over a century ago, the German psychologist Wolfgang Köhler conducted what became a classic experiment. He suspended a banana to keep it just out of reach of a chimpanzee, placing a pile of boxes and crates nearby. The chimp soon stacked up the boxes, climbed them and grabbed the treat.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一个多世纪前，德国心理学家沃尔夫冈·科勒进行了一项经典实验。他把一根香蕉悬挂起来，放在黑猩猩够不到的地方，并在附近放了一堆盒子和板条箱。黑猩猩很快就把盒子堆起来，爬上去抓起食物。

contentParagraphs.en: This was evidence, Köhler believed, of spontaneous problem solving by the chimpanzee; no training was required. It was the kind of thing that humans do all the time.
contentParagraphs.zh: 科勒认为，这是黑猩猩自发解决问题的证据。无需培训。这是人类一直在做的事情。

contentParagraphs.en: Since Köhler's early work, researchers have conducted similar experiments involving an out-of-reach reward and an object to stand upon in birds and elephants. And both have solved the problem successfully.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自从科勒的早期工作以来，研究人员已经在鸟类和大象身上进行了类似的实验，涉及遥不可及的奖励和站立的物体。并且两者都成功解决了问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Olli Loukola, a behavioral ecologist at the University of Turku in Finland wondered whether bumblebees — short-lived creatures with miniscule brains — might be capable of the same task. And in a paper recently published in the journal Science, he and his colleagues present evidence that they are.
contentParagraphs.zh: 芬兰图尔库大学的行为生态学家奥利·卢科拉（Olli Loukola）想知道大黄蜂（大脑很小的短命生物）是否能够完成同样的任务。在最近发表在《科学》杂志上的一篇论文中，他和他的同事提供了证据证明他们确实如此。

contentParagraphs.en: Untrained bumblebees consistently managed to roll a small Styrofoam ball into a position that allowed them to climb atop it to reach a rewarding stimulus overhead.
contentParagraphs.zh: 未经训练的大黄蜂总是设法将一个小泡沫塑料球滚动到一个位置，使它们能够爬到球顶上，从而在头顶上获得有益的刺激。

contentParagraphs.en: "I wasn't expecting that high success rate," Loukola says. He concludes that "very tiny brains can solve super complex problems."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我没想到会有这么高的成功率，”卢科拉说。他的结论是“非常小的大脑可以解决超级复杂的问题。”

contentParagraphs.en: Expecting greatness in the smallest of packages
contentParagraphs.zh: 期待最小封装的伟大

contentParagraphs.en: After studying bumblebees for about a decade, Loukola has come to expect the unexpected. If you don't have limitations on what's possible for them, he says, "you can go wild and crazy and find completely novel stuff."
contentParagraphs.zh: 在对大黄蜂进行了大约十年的研究之后，卢科拉开始期待意想不到的事情发生。他说，如果你对他们的可能性没有限制，“你就可以疯狂地发现完全新颖的东西。”

contentParagraphs.en: His early work proved him right. He showed that bumblebees appeared able to "learn to use tools," he says. "They learn socially from each other; they even understand the role of their partner in cooperative tasks."
contentParagraphs.zh: 他的早期工作证明他是对的。他说，他证明大黄蜂似乎能够“学习使用工具”。 “他们在社交上互相学习；他们甚至了解合作伙伴在合作任务中的作用。”

contentParagraphs.en: Loukola has been drawn to studying tasks long considered the domain of animals with backbones. So he decided to see whether his bumblebees might be capable of a variation on Köhler's classic experiment of the banana and the box.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢科拉一直致力于研究长期以来被认为是有脊椎动物的领域的任务。因此，他决定看看他的大黄蜂是否能够对科勒的香蕉和盒子的经典实验进行变异。

contentParagraphs.en: But he had to replicate the experiment for an organism that could easily fly to reach its reward.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但他必须为一种可以轻松飞行的生物体重复这个实验，以获得奖励。

contentParagraphs.en: Loukola, who was at the University of Oulu in Finland at the time, first trained the bees to associate a small blue circle with a sweet treat. "Bees are super fast in associating things together," he says. "They will learn immediately that blue means reward. Then they start searching for blue stuff."
contentParagraphs.zh: 当时在芬兰奥卢大学工作的卢科拉首先训练蜜蜂将蓝色小圆圈与甜食联系起来。 “蜜蜂将事物联系在一起的速度非常快，”他说。 “他们会立即了解到蓝色意味着奖励。然后他们开始寻找蓝色的东西。”

contentParagraphs.en: He then placed just the blue circle without any sugar water on the ceiling of a hollow puck-shaped container that was about an inch high.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然后，他将没有任何糖水的蓝色圆圈放在一个大约一英寸高的空心冰球形容器的顶部。

contentParagraphs.en: "We designed the arena so that it's just annoyingly [a] little bit too high for them to stand and reach the ceiling," he says, "but too tiny for them to fly."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们设计的竞技场有点太高了，他们无法站立并到达天花板，”他说，“但又太小，无法让他们飞翔。”

contentParagraphs.en: Loukola video recorded his experiments. "With the videos, you can clearly see what is going on," he says.
contentParagraphs.zh: Loukola用视频记录了他的实验。 “通过视频，你可以清楚地看到发生了什么，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: In a recording of the first experiment, a bumblebee is inside the puck alongside a small Styrofoam ball. Remarkably, bee after bee in the video grabs on to the little ball and starts moving it around.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在第一个实验的录音中，一只大黄蜂在冰球内，旁边还有一个小泡沫塑料球。值得注意的是，视频中的一只又一只蜜蜂抓住了小球并开始移动它。

contentParagraphs.en: "Bumblebees, they love rolling balls," says Loukola. "Some of them needed more time and made more errors. But then they continued."
contentParagraphs.zh: “大黄蜂喜欢滚球，”卢科拉说。 “他们中的一些人需要更多时间并犯了更多错误。但他们还是继续了。”

contentParagraphs.en: Eventually, almost three-quarters of the bees moved the ball beneath the blue dot. They then climbed atop the ball, using it like a stepstool to touch the ceiling and reach the otherwise unreachable reward.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最终，几乎四分之三的蜜蜂将球移到了蓝点下方。然后，他们爬到球的顶部，像梯凳一样使用它来接触天花板，并获得原本无法获得的奖励。

contentParagraphs.en: "I planned the experiment so that it's challenging for the bees," he says. "They really need to understand the task in order to solve it."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我计划了这个实验，这样对蜜蜂来说就具有挑战性，”他说。 “他们确实需要了解任务才能解决它。”

contentParagraphs.en: Cognitive flexibility
contentParagraphs.zh: 认知灵活性

contentParagraphs.en: There's an alternative explanation to what motivated success in that first experiment, however. Maybe the bee wasn't purposely directing the ball towards the reward.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，对于第一个实验的成功动机还有另一种解释。也许蜜蜂并没有故意将球引向奖励。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's possible that the bees don't need to understand anything," Loukola allows. "Is this really goal-directed behavior or is this just playing with the balls and solving these tasks by chance?"
contentParagraphs.zh: “蜜蜂可能不需要理解任何东西，”卢科拉承认。 “这真的是目标导向的行为，还是只是玩球并偶然解决这些任务？”

contentParagraphs.en: So in a subsequent experiment, Loukola and his colleagues introduced barriers within the arena to block the blue dot from view. The bee could no longer see the dot unless it maneuvered around the barrier. The ball was then introduced in a different part of the enclosure.
contentParagraphs.zh: 因此，在随后的实验中，卢科拉和他的同事在竞技场内引入了障碍物，以阻挡蓝点。蜜蜂无法再看到这个点，除非它绕过障碍物。然后将球引入外壳的不同部分。

contentParagraphs.en: This time, some 80% of a new batch of bees rolled the ball under the blue circle, convincing Loukola that the bees had solved the problem spontaneously. It's a first, he says, for an insect with a brain the size of a sesame seed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这次，大约 80% 的新一批蜜蜂将球滚到了蓝色圆圈下方，让 Loukola 相信蜜蜂已经自发地解决了问题。他说，对于大脑有芝麻籽大小的昆虫来说，这是第一次。

contentParagraphs.en: "We had this underlying assumption that somehow bigger brains means more powerful computations," says Cat Hobaiter, a primatologist at the University of St. Andrews who wasn't involved in the research. "And so demonstrating this in the bumblebees is really wonderful."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们有一个基本假设，即更大的大脑意味着更强大的计算能力，”未参与这项研究的圣安德鲁斯大学灵长类动物学家 Cat Hobaiter 说。 “所以在大黄蜂身上展示这一点真的很棒。”

contentParagraphs.en: Hobaiter says that this study does a good job replicating similar experiments conducted on animals across the animal kingdom. "Intelligent brains come in really diverse shapes and sizes," she concludes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 霍拜特说，这项研究很好地复制了在整个动物王国的动物身上进行的类似实验。 “智能大脑的形状和大小确实多种多样，”她总结道。

contentParagraphs.en: The cognitive flexibility that Loukola's individual bees demonstrated may pay off in the wild when environmental conditions change suddenly and the insects must collect pollen and nectar no matter what.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当环境条件突然变化并且无论如何昆虫都必须收集花粉和花蜜时，卢科拉个体蜜蜂所表现出的认知灵活性可能会在野外得到回报。

contentParagraphs.en: "Today they might find flowers from here, but tomorrow those flowers are not blooming anymore," says Loukola. "If the workers can flexibly find new ways to get food for the colony, that's the skill that they need to have."
contentParagraphs.zh: “今天他们可能会从这里找到花朵，但明天这些花朵就不再盛开了，”卢科拉说。 “如果工蚁能够灵活地找到新的方法来为蚁群获取食物，这就是他们需要具备的技能。”

contentParagraphs.en: And, Loukola says he has all sorts of future research ideas with bumblebees. He wants to examine their body movements, microgestures and grooming behaviors to see if the insects have a tell preceding their moment of insight. One day, it may even be possible to image the bumblebee brain while it's solving a problem like the one it was presented with here.
contentParagraphs.zh: 而且，卢科拉说他对未来的大黄蜂研究有各种各样的想法。他想检查它们的身体动作、微动作和梳理行为，看看昆虫在洞察之前是否有预知能力。有一天，甚至有可能对大黄蜂在解决像这里提出的问题时的大脑进行成像。

contentParagraphs.en: Loukola knows that more surprises await. The bumblebee
contentParagraphs.zh: 卢科拉知道还有更多惊喜在等待着。大黄蜂
